title: DSDS Document Block — Scoped Unions
description: Defines scoped document block unions, one per artifact type. Each piece of structured docs attached to an artifact is a document block object with a `type` tag. Block kinds include guidelines, anatomy, API specs, variants, states, accessibility, purpose, design specs, principles, scales, motion, content, and interactions. Each artifact type accepts only the relevant block kinds; this scoping prevents attaching irrelevant docs (such as an API spec on a style). Express internal artifact links through the unified `links` array on each entity, not through a dedicated block kind.

generalDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind available on every artifact type. General blocks cover cross-cutting concerns that apply to any artifact.

oneOf.description: Actionable usage rules paired with rationale. Tells the reader what to do (or not do) and why.
Scenario-driven guidance for when to use and when not to use the artifact.
Structured accessibility specs: keyboard interactions, ARIA attributes, screen reader behavior, focus management, color contrast, and motion considerations.
Structured content guidelines: recommended action labels with definitions, usage context, and cross-references, plus localization and i18n considerations.

componentDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on component artifacts. Includes the component-specific kinds (anatomy, API, variants, states, design specs) plus every general kind.

oneOf.description: How to import the component across platforms — package names, import statements, and setup context.
The visual structure of the component: named sub-elements (parts), their design tokens, and optional annotated diagrams.

The code-level interface: properties, emitted events, content slots, CSS custom properties, CSS shadow parts, data attributes, and public methods. For multi-platform components, include one API block per platform.
Events emitted by the component: trigger conditions, return shapes, DOM behavior (bubbling, cancelability, composed), and lifecycle metadata.
All dimensions of visual or behavioral variation. Each axis (size, emphasis, and so on) carries its values, token overrides, previews, and purpose. Optional exclusions document invalid combinations.
All interactive states the component can enter (hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, and the like). Each state carries its triggers, token overrides, and previews.
Measurable visual specs: token inventory, spacing relationships, dimension constraints, typography settings, and responsive behavior.

styleDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on style artifacts. Includes the style-specific kinds (principles, scales, motion) plus every general kind.

oneOf.description: High-level principles that shape decisions for the visual domain: what the system believes and which trade-offs it accepts.
An ordered sequence of values forming a visual scale: a type scale, spacing scale, elevation scale, or similar progression. Each step references a design token.
Named easing curves with cubic-bezier functions (aligned with the DTCG cubicBezier type), recommended durations, and usage guidance for the motion system.

patternDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on pattern artifacts. Includes the pattern-specific kinds (interactions), shared structural kinds (anatomy, variants, states), and every general kind.

oneOf.description: The interaction flow of the pattern: an ordered sequence of steps describing triggers, system responses, and component behavior.
The visual structure of the pattern: named sub-elements (image, title, body, primary action, and so on), their design tokens, and optional annotated diagrams.
Dimensions of variation within the pattern: sub-types, modes, or configurations (e.g., 'no-data', 'user-action', 'error-management'). Optional exclusions document invalid combinations.
States the pattern can enter (populated, empty, loading, error, and so on). Each state carries its triggers, visual changes, and optional previews.
Events emitted during the pattern's interaction flow: trigger conditions, return shapes, and lifecycle metadata.

tokenDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on token and token group artifacts. Tokens use only the general kinds; they do not have component-specific, style-specific, or pattern-specific docs.
